title: Fort Tiracol
tagline: Fort Tiracol — heritage city of Goa

overview.short: Fort Santíssima Trindade (Fortaleza da Santíssima Trindade in Portuguese) also known as Fort Tiracol, is a Portuguese era fort near the village of Tiracol, in the North Goa distric
overview.description: Fort Santíssima Trindade (Fortaleza da Santíssima Trindade in Portuguese) also known as Fort Tiracol, is a Portuguese era fort near the village of Tiracol, in the North Goa district of Goa, India. At the mouth of the Terekhol River, the fort can be reached by a ferry from Querim, 42 km (26 mi) north of Panaji.

topPlaces.name: Tiracol
topPlaces.description: Tiracol is a village on the river right mouth of the Tiracol River and part of the Pernem Sub-Division of North Goa. Due to its location, it is geographically separated from Goa by land making it a Goan exclave in Maharashtra.

topPlaces.name: Redi, Maharashtra
topPlaces.description: Redi is a village in the district of Sindhudurg in Maharashtra. Originally known as Rewati nagar, Redi is located close to the shores of the Arabian Sea.

topPlaces.name: Paliem, Pernem, Goa
topPlaces.description: Paliem is a village in the Pernem Sub-Division of North Goa, India.

topPlaces.name: Terekhol River
topPlaces.description: The Terekhol or Tiracol River is a river in western India. In its upper reaches it is known as the Banda River and in the lower reaches as the Tiracol.

topPlaces.name: Querim Beach
topPlaces.description: Querim Beach is the sandy coastline of the Querim village of Pernem, Goa. It is the largest and northernmost beach in Goa.

topPlaces.name: Querim
topPlaces.description: Querim or Keri is a village on the river mouth of the Tiracol River, part of the Pernem sub-division of North Goa, India. It is the northwesternmost point of the state, known for Querim Beach, the historic Church of St.

topPlaces.name: Ganapati Temple, Redi
topPlaces.description: The Ganapati temple of Redi is located approximately 30 km from Vengurla, in the small village of Redi, Maharashtra, India. The town in which this temple is situated contains iron ore mines and the Ganpati (Ganesha) idol was found in one of the mines near Rewati port in 1976.

topPlaces.name: Yashwantgad Fort
topPlaces.description: Yashwantgad Fort is located in Redi, Maharashtra, near the Maharashtra-Goa border. It sits on a small hill north of Terekhol Creek.

items.note: Tiracol is a village on the river right mouth of the Tiracol River and part of the Pernem Sub-Division of North Goa. Due to its location, it is geographically separated from Goa by land making it a Goan exclave in Maharashtra.
Redi is a village in the district of Sindhudurg in Maharashtra. Originally known as Rewati nagar, Redi is located close to the shores of the Arabian Sea.
Paliem is a village in the Pernem Sub-Division of North Goa, India.
The Terekhol or Tiracol River is a river in western India. In its upper reaches it is known as the Banda River and in the lower reaches as the Tiracol.
Querim Beach is the sandy coastline of the Querim village of Pernem, Goa. It is the largest and northernmost beach in Goa.

Querim or Keri is a village on the river mouth of the Tiracol River, part of the Pernem sub-division of North Goa, India. It is the northwesternmost point of the state, known for Querim Beach, the historic Church of St.

The Ganapati temple of Redi is located approximately 30 km from Vengurla, in the small village of Redi, Maharashtra, India. The town in which this temple is situated contains iron ore mines and the Ganpati (Ganesha) idol was found in one of the mines near Rewati port in 1976.
Yashwantgad Fort is located in Redi, Maharashtra, near the Maharashtra-Goa border. It sits on a small hill north of Terekhol Creek.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Fort Tiracol?
faq.a: The best time to visit Fort Tiracol is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Fort Tiracol?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Manohar International Airport, Mopa (North Goa) (19 km) and the nearest railway station is Vasco da Gama (Goa) (40 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Fort Tiracol?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Fort Tiracol at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Fort Tiracol?
faq.a: Stays in Fort Tiracol start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Fort Tiracol?
faq.a: The top attractions are Tiracol, Redi, Maharashtra, Paliem, Pernem, Goa, Terekhol River, Querim Beach.
